description: Startup WAL replay applies events without guaranteed checkpoint-dirty propagation. Refactor replay API so applying recovered events without registering dirty shards is unrepresentable.
title: P0: Replay must carry checkpoint-dirty effects (unskippable)

description:
**Problem**
Top-level parse/dispatch and command-name wiring still lives primarily in `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/mod.rs` and `crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/mod.rs`, even as handler logic migrates. This keeps ownership ambiguous and adds duplication risk.

**Design**
Move command parse/dispatch ownership into `beads-cli` (or a dedicated `beads-cli` entry surface), keeping `beads-rs` as a minimal orchestration shell that delegates execution.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Primary dispatch table and command-name mapping are owned by `beads-cli`.
- [ ] `beads-rs` entrypoint remains a thin wrapper with explicit compatibility behavior.
- [ ] CLI flags and aliases remain backward compatible.
- [ ] Integration tests confirm unchanged behavior for major command families.
- [ ] Verification gate passes (`fmt`, `dylint`, `clippy`, `test`, `slow-tests`).

**Files**
- crates/beads-cli/src/**
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/mod.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/cli/commands/mod.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/bin/main.rs
title: Move top-level CLI parse/dispatch ownership into beads-cli
